definition: Learning a mapping from inputs to labeled outputs using example pairs; includes classification (discrete labels) and regression (continuous targets).
Finding structure in data without labels, e.g. clustering or dimensionality reduction.
When a model fits training noise and performs poorly on held-out data.
Examples used to fit model parameters.
Held-out data used to tune hyperparameters and estimate generalization during development.
Resampling procedure (e.g. k-fold) to estimate performance with limited data.
An input variable or transformation of raw inputs used by the model.
The target output associated with an input in supervised learning.
